\caption{Deployment-threshold sensitivity of the four adjacent-geometry AURC contrasts in the ten target-adapted conditions. Each cell reports $100\Delta_{.3}/100\Delta_{.5}/100\Delta_{.7}$, where $\Delta=\mathrm{AURC}(\text{left})-\mathrm{AURC}(\text{right})$ and lower AURC is better. Negative values favor the left score. In a condition row, superscript R marks a strict sign reversal relative to the locked $\gamma=0.5$ action; in the macro-mean row, it marks a reversal of the ten-condition macro mean. These are descriptive action sensitivities, not threshold tuning or a robustness guarantee.}

\begin{table*}[t]
\centering
\caption{Descriptive deployment-action sensitivity summaries over the ten target-adapted conditions. Ranges are minimum--maximum across conditions, which are not independent replicates. Accepted-set agreement is the tie-aware fractional Jaccard. Direction is defined by the exact sign of the signed AURC contrast; displayed contrasts and changes are on the $\times100$ scale and no data-dependent tolerance is used. Action quality is reported so that stable rankings cannot conceal a degraded deployed mask. This analysis does not choose a threshold and does not establish a robustness guarantee.}
\label{tab:gamma-action-and-ranking-sensitivity}
\begin{minipage}{0.99\textwidth}
\centering

Predicted-empty rate & $[0.0,\,2.5]\%$ & $[0.0,\,6.5]\%$ & $[0.0,\,22.0]\%$ \\
